prompt: I have a 600-line OrderService class. Its methods include calculateTotal(), sendConfirmationEmail(), updateInventory(), persistToDatabase(), and formatInvoicePdf(). Should I split this class? If so, by which principle and into what?
rubric: Names SRP and frames it as 'one actor' (not 'one reason to change')
Identifies the distinct actors: pricing/sales, customer comms, warehouse/inventory, DBA/persistence, billing/formatting
Proposes a concrete split (OrderTotalCalculator, OrderConfirmation/Notifier, InventoryUpdater, OrderRepository, InvoiceFormatter)
Mentions accidental-duplication or merge-hotspot risk of leaving it as-is
Mentions Facade as optional preservation of a single entry point
